question: Design a `Book` class with private members `title`, `author`, and `isbn` (all strings). Provide public methods to set and get these values, ensuring basic encapsulation.

test_cases.input:
Initial values: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, Douglas Adams, 978-0345391803
Modify title via getter: A New Title
Modify author via getter: append (Modified)
Modify ISBN via getter: replace prefix with XXX
test_cases.expected:
Original Book:
Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Author: Douglas Adams
ISBN: 978-0345391803

Modified Book (via 'robustness issue' getters):
Title: A New Title
Author: Douglas Adams (Modified)
ISBN: XXX-0345391803

errors: The getter methods (getTitle, getAuthor, getIsbn) return a non-const reference to the private member variables. This breaks encapsulation, allowing external code to directly modify the internal state of the Book object without using a designated setter method, which can lead to unexpected behavior and make the class less robust against misuse.
